name: Brazil Nut and Cocoa Parfait
recipeInstructions:
Make the Brazil nut cocoa biscuit:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Roast nuts for 10 minutes, then rub with kitchen towel to remove skins.
2. Line a 9-inch springform pan with foil and prepare the pan (butter and flour).
3. Process toasted nuts and 1/4 cup sugar in a food processor until finely ground; add remaining ingredients and process until well blended.
4. Beat egg yolks and 1/4 cup sugar until pale yellow, then whip egg whites into stiff peaks.
5. Fold the nut mixture over yolk mixture, then fold in whites. Add melted butter and mix gently.
6. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake for 33-38 minutes until clean on a toothpick test. Cool completely.
Make the coffee sauce:
1. Combine crushed beans, half-and-half, and 1/4 cup sugar; bring to gentle boil, then steep 5 minutes.
2. Whisk yolks with remaining sugar, add hot mixture, return to saucepan, and cook gently until thickened but not boiling.
3. Strain into a bowl of ice water and stir until cool; stir in vanilla.
4. Refrigerate until ready.
Make the coffee soaking syrup:
1. Combine cooled espresso, sugar, and rum; set aside.
Make the chocolate mousse:
1. Melt chocolate and butter; let cool.
2. Whip cream with vanilla and rum; chill until needed.
3. Beat eggs, water, salt, and sugar in a bowl over hot water until thick and 160°F on an instant read thermometer.
4. Dissolve gelatin in hot water, then fold egg mixture into chocolate-butter mix. Fold in whipped cream and rum.
5. Set aside; don't refrigerate.
Assemble the parfait:
1. Slice biscuit into 3 layers, place bottom layer in pan, soak with half of coffee syrup, spread reserved mousse on top, add second layer soaked with syrup, spread remaining mousse, cover with plastic wrap, and chill for 4 hours.
Unmold and garnish:
1. Loosen parfait from springform pan, remove side, smooth sides, press crumbs onto the sides and sprinkle more across top.
2. Sprinkle chopped toasted Brazil nuts over top; drizzle coffee sauce on a plate, place parfait in center, and sprinkle cocoa around rim.
